outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Iran missiles hit Dimona and Arad as IDF air defense fails — Netanyahu calls it "a very difficult evening"
outlets.summary: NPR documents the dual Iranian strikes on Arad and Dimona — where air defense failed at Arad, 74 injured — and the emerging US-Israel policy rift over war duration.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Air defense fails in Arad as Iran missile hits Dimona — raising questions about Israeli defense coverage
outlets.summary: CNN covers the Arad interception failure and Dimona strike as evidence that Iran retains significant strike capacity despite US claims of 90% degradation.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Deepening Iran conflict: Netanyahu says 'very difficult evening' as Iran strikes near Israel's nuclear city
outlets.summary: WashPost focuses on the political fallout from the Dimona strike: Iran has for the first time demonstrated both capability and willingness to target Israel's nuclear city.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Iran fires on Israel's nuclear city and Arad — air defense fails — Netanyahu vows to "keep striking enemies"
outlets.summary: Fox News frames the Arad and Dimona strikes as continued Iranian aggression. Emphasizes Netanyahu resolve and IDF probe into air defense failure.

outlets.name: Wall Street Journal
outlets.headline: Israel-Iran war Day 22 PM: Iran missiles breach air defenses at Arad, Dimona — Houthis threaten Red Sea closure
outlets.summary: WSJ documents the dual strikes and the emerging Houthi Bab el-Mandeb threat, noting it could double the shipping crisis beyond Hormuz alone.

outlets.name: NY Post
outlets.headline: Iran hits Israeli nuclear city Dimona — Netanyahu "deeply troubled" as Arad air defense crumbles
outlets.summary: NY Post covers Day 22 evening escalation with emphasis on the air defense failure at Arad and the political significance of Iran striking Dimona.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran strikes Israeli nuclear city Dimona, Arad — Houthis weigh Bab el-Mandeb closure
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera emphasizes the civilian dimension of the Arad and Dimona strikes, the air defense failure, and the Houthi threat to close a second global chokepoint.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Iran missile hits Dimona, injuring dozens near Israel's nuclear site; IAEA says no damage to reactor
outlets.summary: Reuters provides factual coverage of both strikes, noting IAEA confirmation of no nuclear damage but highlighting the precedent of Iran targeting Israel's nuclear city.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: Israel's nuclear city Dimona struck by Iranian missile — air defenses fail at Arad
outlets.summary: BBC covers the dual strikes and IDF investigation into the Arad interception failure as a significant development in the war.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Air defense fails in Arad — 74 hurt; Iran strikes Dimona; IDF opens probe
outlets.summary: Times of Israel live coverage of the Arad interception failure and Dimona strike. IDF probe opened. Netanyahu: "a very difficult evening."

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Iran hits Israeli nuclear city Dimona, breaches Arad air defense — Day 22 most dangerous evening of war
outlets.summary: JPost calls Day 22 evening the most dangerous night of the war so far — dual strikes on Arad and Dimona reveal sustained Iranian ballistic missile capability.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: "Skies of Israel are unprotected" — Ghalibaf after Iran missile strikes near Negev nuclear site
outlets.summary: Haaretz flags Ghalibaf's statement as a threat of further nuclear-area targeting, and examines the political fallout from the interception failure.

outlets.name: IRNA (Iran)
outlets.headline: Iran responds to Natanz attack with precision strikes on Israeli nuclear city Dimona and Arad
outlets.summary: Iranian state media frames the Arad and Dimona strikes as a proportional response to the US-Israeli strike on Natanz, emphasizing Iran's continued deterrence capability.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran's missiles pierce Israel's missile shield — strikes on Dimona, Arad prove Iran's power
outlets.summary: Press TV celebrates the Arad air defense failure and Dimona strike as proof that Iran retains a credible deterrence posture despite 22 days of US-Israeli strikes.

outlets.name: Tasnim News
outlets.headline: IRGC confirms successful strikes on Dimona, Arad — proves Iran retains full missile strike capability
outlets.summary: Tasnim (IRGC-linked) reports IRGC claiming the strikes as demonstration of Iran's undiminished military capability despite US claims of 90% degradation.
